name: Grade 3 Reading — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 3 pathway for close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 3 knowledge and transferable skills in close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

units.title: Investigate: close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 3 task connected to close reading, theme, text structure, and informational research.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
